What I learnt from binge-watching Dylan Wiliam

📅 May 21, 2020

I’ve been binge-watching stuff by Dylan Wiliam over the last few days. He seems to talk a lot of sense and is easy to listen to. He makes the point that there are four broad sets of reasons as to why we educate people:<div><div><ol style="text-align: left;"><li>As preparation for life - to allow them to find their way in the world</li><li>To pass on culture -  what Matthew Arnold calls: ‘… the best that has been known and said in the world, and thus with the history of the human spirit.’</li><li>To prepare for democratic citizenship</li><li>To prepare for the world of work</li></ol></div></div><div>
</div><div>Curriculum</div><div>What educational purposes</div><div>What experiences are needed</div><div>How should these be organised</div><div>Assessment</div><div>
</div><div>The curriculum is a selection for culture</div><div>
</div>
